subject: Ecology
title: Wetland Carbon Storage
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Wetland Carbon Storage.

hook.text: Wetlands cover just 6% of land but store twice as much carbon as all the world's forests.

buildup.text: Waterlogged soils lack oxygen, slowing decomposition and locking dead plants as peat for millennia.

discovery.text: Peatlands alone hold about 600 gigatonnes of carbon—more than all living vegetation combined.

twist.text: Draining wetlands exposes stored carbon to air, releasing it as CO₂ and accelerating climate change.

climax.text: Rewetting drained peatlands is one of the most cost-effective climate mitigation strategies.

punchline.text: Wetlands fight climate change by doing nothing—just staying wet.

quiz.question: How much carbon do peatlands store?
options: About 600 gigatonnes—more than all living vegetation
